description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with a single job named docker-lint that runs in the lint stage, extends the .base_tpl template, and is allowed to fail. The job defines a files_to_lint variable (empty by default) and executes a series of echo commands to print red warning symbols, a DEPRECATED JOB notice, a release note, and a suggestion to use the new job at ${tpl_directory}/job/container/lint.yml, then resets terminal colors. It defines a Bash function _dockerlint that prints a header, runs `docker run --rm -i hadolint/hadolint < $1` to lint a Dockerfile, and prints a green success message. The script then loops over all files matching "Dockerfile*" with `for script in $(find . -name "Dockerfile*"); do _dockerlint $script; done`, loops over any paths in $files_to_lint with `for file in $files_to_lint; do _dockerlint $file; done`, and finally executes `false` to mark the job as failed (but allow_failure permits continuation).
